document.name: preview-dev-gan
document.title: In-preview GAN dev loop: edit the live BFF (HMR) ↔ Playwright visual critic
document.summary: Runs INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview. enter_dev_mode replaces the preview's prod BFF pod with a hot-reload dev-server Sandbox that adopts the preview's Service (so the preview URL serves live edits — P1 'preview-native' mode). Then a generator↔critic GAN loop: the generator edits /sandbox/work/repo/src and runs ./sync.sh to push changes to the live preview (vite HMR, ~seconds), and an independent Playwright-MCP visual critic drives a real browser against the in-cluster preview URL, screenshots the rendered app, and grades it — looping until accepted or the iteration cap. Generator + critic share the per-execution JuiceFS /sandbox/work (interactive-cli family). See docs/agentic-deploy-inspect-loop.md + docs/generator-critic-multi-agent.md.

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Improve the visual design and clarity of the workflow-builder home/dashboard page: a clear visual hierarchy, a cohesive color + spacing system, and an obvious primary call-to-action. Keep it functional and responsive; do not break navigation.
intent.description: What the generator changes in the live BFF UI (edits land under src/ and hot-reload onto the preview).

service.title: Service
service.description: The preview service whose pod is replaced with a dev server (P1 preview-native; workflow-builder = the BFF).

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: Drives Playwright MCP against the live preview URL + grades the UI. interactive-cli family (in-pod Chromium).

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Improve the visual design and clarity of the workflow-builder home/dashboard page: a clear visual hierarchy, a cohesive color + spacing system, and an obvious primary call-to-action. Keep it functional and responsive; do not break navigation.

artifacts.from: ${ { service: (.data.service // ""), url: (.data.url // ""), browseUrl: (.data.browseUrl // ""), syncUrl: (.data.syncUrl // ""), ready: (.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Preview-native dev mode

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "; export REF=" + ((.trigger.repoRef // "main") | @sh) + "; export SUBDIR=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.repoSubdir // ".") | @sh) + "; export PATHS=" + (((.enter_dev_mode.syncPaths // ["src"]) | join(" ")) | @sh) + "; export SYNCURL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.syncUrl // "") | @sh) + ";" + "
set -e
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo
git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "$REF" https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/$REPOURL.git repo
printf 'SUBDIR=%s\nPATHS="%s"\nSYNCURL=%s\n' "$SUBDIR" "$PATHS" "$SYNCURL" > /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cat > /sandbox/work/sync.sh <<'SH'
#!/bin/sh
set -e
. /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cd /sandbox/work/repo/$SUBDIR
SYNCPATHS=""; for p in $PATHS; do [ -e "$p" ] && SYNCPATHS="$SYNCPATHS $p"; done
tar -czf /tmp/sync.tgz $SYNCPATHS
curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/sync.tgz -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNCURL"
echo
echo SYNCED
SH
chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
echo CLONED $(git -C repo rev-parse --short HEAD); echo '--- .syncenv ---'; cat /sandbox/work/.syncenv" }

agentConfig.name: Generator (live BFF dev)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a generator-critic loop editing a LIVE, hot-reloading microservice UI. The repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. EDIT FILES ONLY UNDER /sandbox/work/repo/src (only paths under there are synced). After each batch of edits, run /sandbox/work/sync.sh to push your changes to the live preview — it hot-reloads (vite HMR) in a few seconds. Do NOT run npm install or a build (the preview already runs the dev server). VERIFY your change took by curling the IN-CLUSTER preview URL given in the prompt (NOT the public/browse URL — that is for the human + the critic's browser). An independent visual Critic reviews the RENDERED page each round and returns specific feedback; address it, make the most impactful improvement, run ./sync.sh, then end your turn with a one-line summary. Keep the app functional — do not break routing or the build.

body.prompt:
${ "Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "improve the UI") + "

The repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo; edit ONLY under repo/" + (.enter_dev_mode.repoSubdir // ".") + "/src. After editing, run /sandbox/work/sync.sh to hot-reload the live preview. Verify by curling the IN-CLUSTER preview URL " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + " (do NOT curl the public URL " + (.enter_dev_mode.browseUrl // "") + ").

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

Critic feedback to address now:
" + (if .loop.last.critique then (.loop.last.critique.feedback // (.loop.last.critique | tojson)) else "(none yet — make your first improvement)" end) + "

Implement the change under repo/src, run ./sync.sh, confirm it is live, then end with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: Visual critic (Playwright)
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting, INDEPENDENT visual design critic with strong taste. You do NOT edit the code. Use your Playwright MCP browser tools to OPEN the in-cluster preview URL given in the prompt, take a screenshot, and inspect the RENDERED page (navigate key views, resize to a mobile width if useful). Judge the change against the stated intent on: visual hierarchy & clarity, cohesion (type scale, spacing rhythm, color harmony, AA contrast), distinctiveness (not a generic/AI-default look), and functionality (the page still works, nav intact, no obvious layout breakage at mobile + desktop). CALIBRATION: a PASS means the intent is genuinely well-realized and the page is crafted — not merely 'fine'. DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless it is clearly strong; cite concrete, visible specifics. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "feedback": string}. `feedback` is the prioritized, concrete guidance the generator will act on next round.

body.prompt:
${ "Review the LIVE preview against this change request using your Playwright browser tools.

Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "improve the UI") + "

Open the in-cluster preview URL: " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + " (navigate, screenshot, check mobile + desktop). Then return ONLY the strict JSON verdict {meets_criteria, score, feedback} from your instructions. Be demanding: meets_criteria true ONLY if the intent is clearly well-realized AND the page still works." }

artifacts.title: In-preview GAN result
artifacts.description: Accepted flag, iteration count, final generator summary, and the critic's last verdict.
